subject: [COMMON][DCAE] Allow internal only ports
commit_message:
[COMMON][DCAE] Allow internal only ports

Sometimes, we need to generate a service for ports that are internal
only. So, when the service is "NodePort", it's an issue.
By adding a toggle in service template + marking the ports as internal
only, we'll now create two services (one internal, one external) if
needed.
Adding the option in Ves Collector service.

Issue-ID: OOM-1
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: I27cf48f79dddc849133c27527531efb66cec4162

Patch Set 3:

Instead of adding separate flags (has_internal_only_ports/internal_only), adding Service definition as list will be better option -  something like below

# service configuration
service:  
  - type: ClusterIP
    name: dcae-ves-collector-metric
    ports:
      - port: 4444
        name: metric
  - type: NodePort
    name: dcae-ves-collector
    ports:
      - name: http
        port: 8443
        plain_port: 8080
        port_protocol: http
        nodePort: 17
        useNodePortExt: true	

This is more readable and allows user to specify svc name explicitly (this could be useful beyond metric case).  Any thoughts?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> Instead of adding separate flags (has_internal_only_ports/internal_only), adding Service definition as list will be better option -  something like below

>     name: dcae-ves-collector
>     ports:
>       - name: http
>         port: 8443
>         plain_port: 8080
>         port_protocol: http
>         nodePort: 17
>         useNodePortExt: true	
> 
> This is more readable and allows user to specify svc name explicitly (this could be useful beyond metric case).  Any thoughts?

just seen this one
indeed it can be good.
I still have to manage "list" and "dict" case but it's doable I think!
